test.command: docker compose exec -T funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -x'
test.details: 188 tests passed. One test failure was found (test_traversal_request_returns_fallback) and fixed by adding catch-all route in placeholder branch of main.py.

typecheck.command: docker compose exec -T funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && ty check .'

lint.command: docker compose exec -T funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && ruff check . && ruff format --check . && cd /app/frontend && pnpm check && pnpm format:check'
lint.details: Ruff check, Ruff format, svelte-check (0 errors, 0 warnings), and Prettier all passed

appliedUpdates.description: Added subprocess-conventions.md documenting the pattern for external tool invocation (subprocess.run with shell=False, timeout, error handling)
Fixed placeholder SPA branch in main.py to include catch-all route for non-root paths, ensuring SPA fallback behavior works even without a built frontend

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the 'Frontend State Management (Svelte 5)' section to show both the class-based singleton pattern and the module-level getter pattern as acceptable approaches. Current example only shows class-based pattern (`class Foo { bar = $state(0); } export const foo = new Foo()`), but upload store uses module-level `let state = $state(...)` with exported getter functions, which is equally valid for singletons.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2-upload-ui feature uses module-level state with getters (upload.svelte.ts). AGENTS.md only documents class-based pattern. Both patterns work correctly.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the 'Coding Conventions > Backend (Python)' section to note that the actual pattern for DATA_DIR and similar config is module-level constants (`DATA_DIR = get_settings().data_dir`) with test fixtures monkey-patching the module attribute, not DI via FastAPI Depends as currently stated ('Use plain sqlite3 with connection management via FastAPI Depends'). This pattern is used consistently across main.py, api/system.py, and services/upload.py.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2-upload-endpoint review noted the discrepancy. Three modules use module-level config. The AGENTS.md reference to 'FastAPI Depends' for DB connections describes a pattern that doesn't match the actual implementation.

rejectedObservations.reason: Implementation detail, not a convention that needs documentation. Works correctly as-is.
